title: Amaran
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}

The '''Amarans''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] characterized by both [[vulpine]] and [[Canine/Legends|canine]] characteristics. The Amaran anatomy included pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]]; a narrow, bewhiskered [[Nose/Legends|snout]]; a [[Hair/Legends|furry]] body; a bipedal stance; and a bushy tail. As unrepentant hagglers, Amarans made natural [[Merchant/Legends|traders]], especially of non-sentient beasts, which they [[Beast charmer|tamed and bred]].

Natives of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Amar]], the Amarans eventually emigrated to worlds such as [[Vulpter/Legends|Vulpter]] and [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. On the former planet, the vulpines served as managers of underground storage facilities owned by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] cartel and staffed by workers drawn from the planet's native [[Vulptereen/Legends|Vulptereen]] population. On Naboo, on the other hand, Amarans captured, tamed, and sold creatures such as the [[Bursa/Legends|bursa]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Amarans were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of small,<ref name="CATCW" /> [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] bipeds<ref name="CSWE I 26" /> who were considered both [[vulpine]]<ref name="WOSW" /> and [[Canine/Legends|canine]] in nature. They had four clawed digits per [[Limb/Legends|limb]] and walked digitigrade upon their toes. Each member of the species had a bushy tail. The [[black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] gazed forward, and a pointed muzzle held a red mouth and narrowed to a small, black [[Nose/Legends|nose]]<ref name="CSWE I 26" /> surrounded by whiskers.<ref name="G5">{{Topps|set=2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5|cardname=Artist Sketch Cards by [[Robin Pronovost]]}}</ref> Two large, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]] stood erect at the top of the head.<ref name="WOSW" />

Each Amaran was covered in variegated [[Hair/Legends|fur]]. One possible pattern paired red-orange fur across the back, tail, and outside of the arms and legs with white patches on the insides of the limbs and ears, on the snout, on the soles of the feet, and across the neck and belly. The fur grew short on most of the body with the exceptions of the tail and of tufted jowls.<ref name="WOSW" />

==Society and culture==
Amarans were well-known for their propensity to bargain and bicker over transactions.<ref name="CSWE I 26" /> Some Amarans were known as [[beast charmer]]s; a typical member of the profession might carry a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] in the course of [[Gender/Legends|his or her]] duties.<ref name="G5" /> The species wore clothing items such as trousers, vests, shirts,<ref name="WOSW" /> [[Piercing/Legends|earrings]], and [[Necklace/Legends|necklaces]].<ref name="G5" />

==Amarans in the galaxy==
[[File:Amaran_bursa_deal.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Amaran animal dealers operated on planets ranging from Naboo to Coruscant.]]
The Amarans evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Amar]],<ref name="CSWE I 26" /> which was situated in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Airon sector/Legends|Airon sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Amar system/Legends|Amar system]]</ref> By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Clone Wars, Amarans had begun emigrating from Amar to other worlds. During the period in which the planet [[Vulpter/Legends|Vulpter]] was under [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] control, six percent of the world's 421 million inhabitants—or 25.3 million individuals—were members of the Amaran species. The management staff of Trade Federation–owned underground storage tunnels on the planet was drawn entirely from Vulpter's Amaran population; the facilities were otherwise staffed by the planet's native species, the [[Vulptereen/Legends|Vulptereens]]. The arrangement caused tension between the two species, since the Vulptereen laborers felt they were ill compensated for their hard labor in the subterranean locales.<ref name="BATDC 4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050428planet4|series=Byss and the Deep Core|text=Part 4: Vulpter}}</ref>

During the regime of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], some Amarans had established themselves in the [[Gungan Swamp]] on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. There, the Amarans captured wild [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]] for breeding and off-world [[Merchant/Legends|sales]].<ref name="WOSW" /> They plied this trade on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] as well, with Amaran dealers constituting a major segment of the [[Coruscant Livestock Exchange and Exhibition]], where they were known to have a wide selection of hard-to-find creatures.<ref name="CATCW" /> [[Unidentified Amaran bursa dealer|One such dealer]] negotiated with a [[Unidentified Pacithhip bursa buyer|Pacithhip]] who was interested in reselling a bursa to [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]]; meanwhile, a smaller [[Unidentified Amaran child|Amaran]] spoke with a [[Unidentified Veknoid (Coruscant)|Veknoid]].<ref name="WOSW" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Canon development===
{{Quote|Another of [Robin's] designs for the Essential Guide, the fox-like Amarans, also made it into the Wildlife book.|Daniel Wallace, regarding the origin of the Amarans|<ref name="danwallace">{{Blog|danwallace|34|Lostwords: The Essential Guide to Episode I}}</ref>}}
[[File:Amaran_concept_art.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Robin Pronovost's concept art of the Amarans served as the inspiration for Terryl Whitlatch's version of the species.]]
Author and illustrator [[Robin Pronovost]] designed the Amarans for a work known as ''[[The Essential Guide to Episode I]]'' at the request of its author, [[Daniel Wallace]],<ref name="danwallace" /> for a species of [[Fox (creature)/Legends|fox]] [[Alien/Legends|aliens]]. The pair's inspiration for the name was "Amara," Pronovost's fox [[Wikipedia:Fursona|fursona]]<ref name="20th Anniversary">{{Twitter|artyewok|status/1387798855668965384|Robin Pronovost|archivedate=20210925084729|quote=Happy 20th Anniversary to The Wildlife of Star Wars and, by association, the birth of the Amarans, the fox aliens I designed. Here is a hilarious story about their genesis. A lot of this is in the wookieepedia article, but without my ADHD rambling. Strap in folks. 1/?}} {{C|thread}}</ref> that had been named after the character [[W:c:marvel:Amara Aquilla (Earth-616)|Amara]] from the comic book series [[W:c:marvel:New Mutants Vol 1|''The New Mutants'']].<ref name="Amara">{{Twitter|artyewok|status/1378347742368567298|Robin Pronovost|archivedate=20210403140537|quote=Fun fact, I named Amara after Amara from the New Mutants comics because I am a gigantic nerd.}}</ref> Although ''The Essential Guide to Episode I'' was ultimately canceled, artist [[Terryl Whitlatch]] was inspired by Pronovost's concept art to include the Amarans in ''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]'', which was published by [[Chronicle Books]] in [[2001]].<ref name="FA">{{WebCite|url=https://flyingarmadillo.com/amarans-backstory-and-history|text=Amarans Backstory and History|author=[[Robin Pronovost]]|date=2010-01-31|work=[https://www.flyingarmadillo.com Art by Robin Pronovost]|archivedate=20210123114514}}</ref>

Pronovost included Amaran characters in their artist sketch cards for ''[[2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5]]'', which was published by [[Topps]] in [[2010]].<ref name="G5" />

===Authorial intent===
{{Quote|Seeing as there is canon lacking, please feel free to use these ideas in your roleplaying game sessions as jump starters….|Robin Pronovost, on their unofficial take on the Amaran species|<ref name="FA" />}}
On [[January 31]], 2010, Pronovost presented their personal description of the Amarans—with the caveat that the ideas were non-[[canon]] until used in a licensed source—for the use of ''Star Wars'' fans who wished to add such characters to their role-playing scenarios. According to Pronovost, members of the species were relatively diminutive, standing between 3.5 and 4 [[Foot/Legends|feet]] in height. Each Amaran tended toward a wiry frame, and his or her long tail acted as a counterweight that afforded great dexterity. The species's fur came in colors as diverse as white, orange, black, yellow-orange, and gray, with white and gray the most common. Their eyes, meanwhile, were generally black, although green, blue, and red were also known. Male Amarans tended to be slightly larger than females. Amaran mothers generally bore one pup at a time; only when [[Breast/Legends|breast]]-feeding [[Youngling/Legends|young]] did female Amarans exhibit obvious breasts. An Amaran was considered an adult at age eighteen.<ref name="FA" />

[[File:Amaran_Galaxy_sketch_card.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Pronovost included Amarans in their sketch cards for ''2010 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 5''.]]
Pronovost's ideas about Amaran society and culture state that Amaran religion revered the [[Star/Legends|stars]] and other celestial objects, such as their world's [[Moon/Legends|moon]], Rurrri. An Amaran myth told of Rurrri being lonesome and venturing to Amar in the form of a white fox. He cried all night, so the sun, Surru, manifested as a gold-furred Amaran to keep Rurrri company. The two [[Reproduction/Legends|mated]], and their offspring became the stars. The species that evolved tended toward frivolity and canniness, traits they put to work in careers such as trading, [[Pilot/Legends|flying]] vehicles, and conning unsuspecting targets. They were naturally comfortable with non-sentient animals, and the species was known for its [[Animal trainer|animal handlers]]. The world's beast-tamers were able to capture and tame even large creatures, including the Vrrryyyk, a flying beast with both [[Bird/Legends|bird]] and [[Reptile/Legends|reptile]] characteristics that served as the preferred Amaran means of transportation. They had a love for expensive food, drink, and clothing. The species maintained their tails in top order through grooming, as the tail was a source of pride.<ref name="FA" />

The Amarans, according to Pronovost, took great pains to preserve the natural environment of their homeworld, a planet that abounded with life; in Amaran culture, [[Hunting/Legends|poaching]] was considered a heinous crime. Members of the species congregated in [[City/Legends|cities]]. [[Mining/Legends|Mining]] was a large industry on Amar, but the Amarans were careful to conduct such operations through non-destructive means whenever possible. Amar's exports included artwork, foodstuffs, creatures, [[Ore/Legends|ores]], cloth, and [[energy crystal]]s.<ref name="FA" />

Pronovost's notes explain that reverence for the stars prompted the Amarans to develop a primitive form of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel on their own. With the advance, they discovered the planet Naboo, a world that served as their gateway to the larger [[galactic community]]. Amarans who emigrated from their homeworld often served as merchants, confidence people, [[Gambling/Legends|gamblers]], and tourists. A large population of Amarans moved to Naboo, where they sold local tamed creatures to offworlders as well as the planet's natives, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]], and the non-Human [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. The Amarans of Naboo grew wealthy from their swamp settlements and even saved the Naboo bursa from extinction at the hands of Gungan hunters. Some Amarans exhibited [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]], but only a few became [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] due to the foreignness of the discipline required by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]; accordingly, Amaran Jedi were seen as odd by their non-Jedi fellows.<ref name="FA" />
